name: Beneath the Sea
description: Oceans, sea creatures, and deep-sea mysteries

questions.q: How many arms does an octopus have?

source.notes: Technically called 'arms', not tentacles.

questions.q: An orca is more commonly known as what kind of whale?

source.notes: Largest member of the dolphin family.

questions.q: A group of dolphins is called a what?

source.notes: Also used for whales.

questions.q: SpongeBob SquarePants lives in what fictional underwater town?

source.notes: Based beneath the real-world Bikini Atoll in the Pacific.

questions.q: A starfish (sea star) notably lacks which organ?

source.notes: They have a nerve net instead of a centralized brain.

questions.q: Clown fish are famous for living symbiotically within the tentacles of what?

source.notes: A mutualistic relationship; clownfish are immune to the anemone's sting.

questions.q: How many hearts does an octopus have?

source.notes: Two branchial hearts for the gills, one systemic heart for the body.

questions.q: Which French oceanographer co-invented the Aqua-Lung — the first widely available SCUBA equipment?

source.notes: Developed with engineer Émile Gagnan in 1943.

questions.q: The popular seafood dish 'calamari' is made from which animal?

source.notes: From Italian 'calamaro'.

questions.q: The oarfish — the world's longest bony fish — most closely resembles what other sinuous animal?

source.notes: Can grow over 8 m long.

questions.q: Shrimp, crabs, and lobsters all belong to which order of crustaceans?

source.notes: 'Decapoda' means 'ten-footed'.

questions.q: A baby walrus is called what?

source.notes: Calves can weigh up to 75 kg at birth.

questions.q: The blue-ringed octopus, small but deadly, is known for what trait?

source.notes: Its venom contains tetrodotoxin; there is no known antivenom.

questions.q: The wreck of the USS Arizona, sunk in 1941, lies in Pearl Harbor in which ocean?

source.notes: Sunk December 7, 1941; now a national memorial.

questions.q: What is the scientific name for the study of fish?

source.notes: From Greek 'ikhthus' (fish).

questions.q: Dolphins, whales, and porpoises are classified as which kind of animal?

source.notes: Mammals of the order Cetacea; they breathe air and nurse their young.

questions.q: Which country's coast is home to the Great Barrier Reef?

source.notes: Stretches over 2,300 km along Queensland's coast; world's largest coral reef system.

questions.q: A lobster's blood is what color, due to its copper-based hemocyanin?

source.notes: Colorless/clear normally, turns pale blue when exposed to oxygen.

questions.q: What is the name of the deepest known point in the world's oceans?

source.notes: Challenger Deep in the Mariana Trench reaches almost 11,000 m below sea level.

questions.q: European eels migrate thousands of miles to spawn in which Atlantic sea?

source.notes: Bordered by ocean currents, not land — the only sea without a coast.

questions.q: The first nuclear-powered submarine, the USS Nautilus, was launched in what year?

source.notes: Launched January 21, 1954; first vessel to reach the North Pole submerged.

questions.q: Which body of water has the highest salinity (salt concentration) in the world?

source.notes: Dead Sea is about 34% salt — nearly 10 times saltier than the open ocean.
